name: Apps
summary: Scribe, Veridia, and CloudClinic app/interface surfaces that consume mediated Core, GOS, Session Runtime, AACI, and MSR state.
invariants: Apps are interfaces, not law engines.
Apps consume mediated surfaces and do not own Core law, GOS policy, or clinical authority.
App surfaces must avoid direct raw clinical data exposure and direct identifiers.
Native macOS UI remains scaffold/foundation maturity until explicitly implemented and validated.
allowedWork: Maintain app-safe mediated surfaces for Scribe, Veridia, and CloudClinic.
Add or repair smoke-testable app executable paths.
Align app docs with interface doctrine and macOS 26+ Liquid Glass baseline.
Validate app changes through Swift tests and app smoke commands.
forbiddenWork: Claim final UI delivery without implementation.
Expose raw direct identifiers or unmediated clinical payloads.
Give apps clinical, regulatory, signature, prescription, referral, or finalization authority.
knownGaps: APP-011 remains READY for Veridia session path work.
APP-012 remains READY for CloudClinic session path work.
Final app UI shells are not implemented.
notes: This territory covers app/interface boundaries, not product task completion.
APP-013 established Veridia as the canonical patient app name (2026-05-04). All future work uses Veridia naming.
ST-011 does not change APP-011 or APP-012 status.
